attacking_style: Launches attacks on steep gradients (12-13% ramps) with 3-4km remaining, particularly effective on short punchy climbs and uphill finishes of 300m. Timing occasionally premature when facing pure finishers.
team_tactics: Operates with limited domestique support; Groupama team shows inexperience at Grand Tours. Often positioned for solo opportunism rather than controlled team leadouts. Relies on individual moves from breakaways or reduced groups.
terrain_preference: Excels on short explosive climbs (300m uphill finishes) and punchy steep ramps (12-13%+). Thrives in cold/rainy conditions where hesitation creates openings. Struggles in individual time trials and sustained tempo climbs.
vulnerability: Individual TT remains critical weakness (dropped 2+ minutes). Cracks when pace is controlled by strong teams (INEOS tempo). Decision-making under pressure questionable (tactical miscalculations at intermediate sprints).
key_patterns: Wins from reduced breakaways with late attacks 3-4km out, exploiting hesitation in cold/wet conditions (2 wins)
Superior climbing acceleration on short steep finishes overcomes poor positioning (Romandie, Châtel victories)
Repeatedly attacks rivals for GC gains even from difficult positions, persistent aggression (MARTÍNEZ, solo moves)
Time trial deficit of 2+ minutes consistently undermines GC ambitions despite climbing strength
Strong bike-throw and tactical awareness at finish line (stole Châtel win from celebrating VAN AERT)
Form vulnerable to illness mid-stage race, loses performance trajectory after strong early showing
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed mixed signals—podium at Paris-Nice but tactical errors, strong Dauphiné climbing negated by illness and TT struggles. Tour chase group positive but limited breakthrough.
